differences.title: 1. REACTIVITY MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Updates only affected parts without virtual DOM. Extremely efficient.
differences.rightDesc: Tracks state changes within components. Efficient re-renders.

differences.title: 2. COMPILATION
differences.leftDesc: Compiles to efficient JavaScript. No runtime overhead.
differences.rightDesc: Compiles components into imperative code. Zero runtime.

differences.title: 3. VIRTUAL DOM
differences.leftDesc: Direct DOM manipulation for max performance and minimal bundle size.
differences.rightDesc: Compiles away virtual DOM. Direct DOM updates.

differences.title: 4. API STYLE
differences.leftDesc: Emphasizes JavaScript functions and JSX. Familiar to React devs.
differences.rightDesc: Uses HTML-like syntax. Intuitive and easy to learn.

differences.title: 5. REACTIVITY PRIMITIVES
differences.leftDesc: Core primitives: signal, effect, memo. Explicit dependency tracking.
differences.rightDesc: Stores for global state, component state. Built-in transitions.

differences.title: 6. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Benchmarks consistently show leading performance due to fine-grained reactivity.
differences.rightDesc: Very fast due to compilation, minimal runtime.

differences.title: 7. COMMUNITY SIZE
differences.leftDesc: Smaller but highly active and engaged developer base.
differences.rightDesc: Larger, established community with extensive resources.
